prevalence.trend: Stable to slightly decreasing due to improved risk factor management

incidence.source: ESO Atlas of Stroke in Europe 2023

burden.source: WHO Europe Global Burden of Disease 2023; ESO Reports

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (e.g., sudden focal neurological deficit) confirmed by neuroimaging (CT/MRI); NIH Stroke Scale for severity; ESO guidelines emphasize rapid imaging to differentiate ischemic vs. hemorrhagic stroke

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Risk factor control ~60% (e.g., BP management per ESO targets)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Aligned with ESO recommendations: Urgent CT head within 20 minutes of arrival; MRI for confirmation if needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ESO Guidelines 2021: IV thrombolysis within 4.5 hours, mechanical thrombectomy up to 24 hours in select cases; secondary prevention with antiplatelets/statins
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Disparities in access across Eastern vs. Western EU; stroke unit availability varies (80-90% coverage); aging population increases burden
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High thrombolysis rates in Western EU (~15-20%); ESO-led networks for stroke registries and research; integrated EU-wide prevention campaigns
